findings.evidence: <main> at line 27 contains another <main> at line 28. Only one <main> landmark is valid per document.

findings.description: Duke Energy logo with 'BUILDING A SMARTER ENERGY FUTURE' tagline missing from the title page in the HTML conversion.
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 displays the Duke Energy logo and tagline 'BUILDING A SMARTER ENERGY FUTURE' prominently centered at the bottom of the title slide. The HTML title page section transitions directly from the airboat photo into the 'Our Role' section without rendering this organizational logo.

findings.description: The URL 'duke-energy.com/AquaticPlants' appears as plain text without an <a href> wrapper in two places in the HTML, whereas the PDF shows it styled as a clickable hyperlink (teal/underlined text).
findings.evidence:
In the HTML, the 'New Reporting Tool' section has '

duke-energy.com/AquaticPlants

' as plain text. The Key takeaways list also has 'duke-energy.com/AquaticPlants' as plain text. The PDF presents both instances in styled teal/underlined hyperlink formatting.

findings.description: Alt text for one partner logo is inaccurate. The PDF clearly shows a logo reading 'CHARLOTTE WATER NORTH CAROLINA', but the HTML alt text describes it as 'Charlotte-Mecklenburg Storm Water Services logo', which is a different organization.
findings.evidence: HTML: . PDF page 2 shows a logo with the text 'CHARLOTTE WATER' and 'NORTH CAROLINA' beneath it. Charlotte Water and Charlotte-Mecklenburg Storm Water Services are distinct entities.

findings.description: The 'How to Take a Good ID Photo' slide uses green borders for good photo examples and red borders for bad photo examples, but the alt text for both image groups does not convey this critical good-vs-bad distinction that is the entire point of the slide.
findings.evidence: HTML alt texts: 'Examples of aquatic plants in water and on shore' and 'Close-up examples of individual aquatic plants held in a hand or floating'. The PDF slide clearly uses green borders (good) and red borders (bad) to teach users the difference between useful ID photos and unhelpful ones, but neither alt text mentions this distinction.
